items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 5 Jun:A strong US jobs report sends bonds/stocks lower
items.summary: US stocks close sharply lower. The NASDAQ index has it worse trading day since April 2025 Meta is considering raising tens of billion dollars in a stock offering Trump: Would like to see lower interest rates Baker Hughes oil rig count up 2 to 431 Gold is sinking with the US dollar rising and yields moving higher Traders push bitcoin to the lowest level going back to October 2024. Stocks reach new lows CNN: Iran-US talks reportedly deadlocked Energy Sec Wright: We will refill the SPR by 40M barrels after Iran conflict is over Trump cheers Jobs report. Bond yields rising/ stock sliding send warnings Tech meltdown deepens while consumer defensive stocks shine bright Fed's Hammack: Today's jobs report reaffirms that the jobs market is roughly in balance Bitcoin nears the lows of the year and the technical picture is ugly if $60,000 breaks Canada employment change 87.8 K vs 10.0 estimate. The unemployment rate falls to 6.6% US May non-farm payrolls +172K vs +85K expected investingLive European markets wrap: A mixed mood waiting on NFP, US-Iran developments The North American employment picture received a significant boost today as both the United States and Canada delivered much stronger-than-expected labor market reports, reinforcing the view that economic activity remains resilient despite concerns about slowing growth and elevated interest rates. In the United States, nonfarm payrolls increased by 172,000 in May, nearly double the consensus estimate of 85,000. Adding to the strength, prior months were revised higher by a combined 93,000 jobs, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% and wage growth remained firm. The report suggested that hiring momentum remains intact and reduced expectations that the Federal Reserve will be in a position to ease policy anytime soon. Treasury yields surged following the release, the U.S. dollar strengthened, and equity markets came under pressure as investors repriced the outlook for interest rates. Canada's labor market also surprised to the upside. Employment rose by 87,800 jobs versus expectations for a gain of just 10,000, while the unemployment rate fell sharply to 6.6% from 6.9%. The strength was particularly encouraging because it was driven by a surge of 154,000 full-time jobs, offsetting weakness seen earlier in the year. Job gains were broad-based, led by construction, transportation and warehousing, accommodation and food services, information and recreation, and manufacturing. The primary area of weakness remained wholesale and retail trade. Taken together, the reports painted a picture of two labor markets that remain far more resilient than expected. That is the good news. The not so good news for policymakers, is that the stronger employment data reduces pressure for additional monetary easing. In the U.S., markets pushed Treasury yields higher and increased expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates elevated for longer and perhaps raise rates toward the end of the year (that would be a big reversal from just a few months ago). While in Canada the report reinforced expectations that the Bank of Canada may remain on hold after its recent easing cycle. Currency markets reflected the stronger Canadian data, with USDCAD moving modestly lower following the release, although gains in the U.S. dollar from the stronger U.S. report limited the downside. The stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report sparked a sharp selloff in the Treasury market as traders reduced expectations for near-term Federal Reserve rate cuts. The move was led by the front end of the yield curve, reflecting a repricing of Fed policy expectations. The 2-year Treasury yield climbed 10.0 basis points to 4.15%, while the 5-year yield rose 7.9 basis points to 4.268. Longer-term yields also moved higher, with the benchmark 10-year yield increasing 5.5 basis points to 4.530% and the 30-year bond yield advancing 2.0 basis points to 4.996%. The steeper rise in shorter-dated yields highlighted the market's view that a resilient labor market and still-elevated inflation pressures could keep the Federal Reserve on hold for longer than previously anticipated.Stocks were mixed to start the day with the Dow higher and the S&P and Nasdaq lower (Nasdaq was down about 300 points going into the jobs report). The jobs report sent the stocks lower on the back up in yields Concerns about the events of the week with Alphabets floating of $85 billion of equity a reminder that AI is going to cost a lot, and that cost is now eating into shareowners value as equity gets diluted. In the past, stock owners benefited from buybacks of shares reversing dilution.. Now with the number of shares increasing, that idea is reversing The declines started to accelerate with both the S&P and NASDAQ indices closed closing below their 200 hour moving averages for the first time since April 2026. For the S&P index the 200 hour moving average comes in at 7404.33. The closing price was 7383.73. For the NASDAQ index the 200 hour moving averages at 26069.49 with a closing price well below that level at 25709.43. There were a number of losers which fell over 10% today including: In a unique week, Marvel Technology was one of the worst performers today with a decline of -16.74%, but one of the best performers for the week with a gain of 28.52%. Indicative of the craziness, it's stock is still up 210% for the year. The stock price this week reached a $324.20 before closing today at $263.47. The USD was stronger today with the AUD and the NZD the hardest hit vs the greenback. Below is an end of week video, outlining the technicals for those two pairs as the trading week comes to an end. Ranking the major currencies losses versus the greenback showed JPY -0.17% CAD -0.19% GBP -0.60% EUR -0.78% NZD -1.19% AUD -1.23% The price of gold reacted negatively to the higher yields and the higher dollar. Gold tumbled $147.17 or -3.29% for its worst day since March 20. For the week the price fell -4.614% Silver fell by $-6.02 or -8.15% (its worst day since May 15). For the week the price fell -9.837% Bitcoin continued its move to the downside fell more than 16% this week its worst one week % decline since October 2022 Recall from yesterday, Treasury Secretary Bessent remarked that he wished the employment report had been released a day earlier. While he denied having any advance knowledge of the numbers, the comment looks particularly interesting in hindsight. Ironically, what would normally be considered good news for the economy turned out to be bad news for the market. The stronger-than-expected jobs report sent Treasury yields sharply higher as investors reassessed the likelihood of near-term Fed rate cuts. The result was a broad stock market selloff, with high-flying technology and AI shares leading the decline. It raises an interesting question: Did some insiders have a rough day today? The markets will next prepare for Kevin Warsh's first meeting as the Fed chair, but before then, the CPI data will be released next week with expectations for a core gain of 0.5% and the YoY rising to 2.9% from 2.8%. The headline is expected to reach 4.2% from 3.8% last month. The Bank of Canada is expected to keep rates unchanged but with the strong jobs report it will be interesting to see if there is a shift. The ECB will also meet and the market has priced a 25 basis point hike. That has been pretty well telegraphed from policy makers already. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美股收盘大幅下挫。纳斯达克指数创下自2025年4月以来的最差单日表现。Meta正考虑通过股票发行筹集数百亿美元。特朗普：希望看到利率下调。贝克休斯石油钻井平台数量增加2座，至431座。随着美元走强和收益率上升，金价持续下跌。交易员将比特币推至2024年10月以来的最低水平。股指创下新低。CNN报道：据称伊朗与美国谈判陷入僵局。能源部长赖特表示：伊朗冲突结束后，我们将向战略石油储备补充4000万桶原油。特朗普对就业报告表示欢迎。债券收益率上升/股市下跌发出警告 科技股崩盘加剧，而消费防御类股表现亮眼 美联储的哈马克：今天的就业报告再次证实，就业市场大致处于平衡状态 比特币逼近年内低点，若跌破6万美元，技术面将十分严峻 加拿大就业人数变动为8.78万，高于10.0万的预期。 美国5月失业率降至6.6%，非农就业人数增加17.2万，远超预期8.5万 investingLive欧洲市场综述：市场情绪涨跌互现，静待非农数据及美伊局势发展 今日北美就业形势获得显著提振，美国和加拿大均发布了远超预期的劳动力市场报告，这进一步印证了尽管存在经济增长放缓和利率走高的担忧，但经济活动依然保持韧性的观点。美国5月非农就业人数增加17.2万人，几乎是市场普遍预期的8.5万人的两倍。更令人振奋的是，此前数月的数据也被上调了共计9.3万个就业岗位，而失业率维持在4.3%不变，工资增长也保持强劲。该报告表明就业增长势头依然强劲，并降低了市场对美联储近期可能放松政策的预期。数据发布后，美国国债收益率飙升，美元走强，股市承压，投资者正重新评估利率前景。加拿大的劳动力市场数据也超预期向好。 就业岗位增加了87,800个，远超预期仅增加10,000个的水平，而失业率则从6.9%大幅降至6.6%。这一强劲表现尤为令人鼓舞，因为它主要得益于全职岗位激增154,000个，抵消了今年早些时候出现的疲软态势。就业增长呈现全面态势，主要由建筑业、运输和仓储业、住宿和餐饮服务业、信息和娱乐业以及制造业带动。批发和零售业仍是表现最疲软的领域。综合来看，这些报告描绘了一幅远比预期更具韧性的劳动力市场图景。这是好消息。对政策制定者而言，不太好的消息是，强劲的就业数据减轻了进一步宽松货币政策的压力。在美国，市场推高了国债收益率，并加剧了市场预期，即美联储将维持较高利率更长时间，甚至可能在年底前加息（这将与仅数月前的预期形成巨大反转）。而在加拿大，该报告强化了市场预期，即加拿大央行在近期宽松周期后可能维持利率不变。 外汇市场反映了加拿大强劲的数据表现，数据发布后美元兑加元小幅走低，不过受美国强劲就业报告提振的美元升值限制了其跌幅。强于预期的美国就业报告引发了国债市场的抛售潮，交易员下调了对美联储近期降息的预期。此次波动主要由收益率曲线短端引领，反映了市场对美联储政策预期的重新定价。2年期国债收益率攀升10.0个基点至4.15%，而5年期收益率上涨7.9个基点至4.268%。长期收益率也出现上涨，基准10年期国债收益率上升5.5个基点至4.530%，30年期国债收益率上升2.0个基点至4.996%。短期收益率的涨幅更为显著，这凸显了市场的观点：强劲的劳动力市场和依然高企的通胀压力可能导致美联储维持利率不变的时间比此前预期更长。股市开盘涨跌不一，道琼斯指数上涨，标普500指数和纳斯达克指数下跌（在就业报告发布前，纳斯达克指数下跌约300点）。 就业报告发布后，受收益率上升影响，股市应声下挫。本周Alphabet公司发行850亿美元股票的事件引发了市场担忧，这提醒人们人工智能将耗资巨大，而随着股权被稀释，这笔成本正在侵蚀股东价值。过去，股东曾受益于股票回购，从而抵消了稀释效应。如今随着流通股数量增加，这一局面正在逆转。随着标普500指数和纳斯达克指数自2026年4月以来首次双双收于200小时移动平均线下方，跌幅开始加速。标普500指数的200小时移动平均线位于7404.33点。收盘价为7383.73点。纳斯达克指数的200小时移动平均线为26069.49点，而收盘价25709.43点远低于该水平。今日有多只个股跌幅超过10%，包括： 在这个不同寻常的一周里，Marvel Technology今日表现垫底，跌幅达-16.74%，但本周却是表现最佳的个股之一，涨幅达28.52%。其疯狂程度可见一斑，该股今年以来累计涨幅仍高达210%。本周股价曾触及324.20美元，今日收盘报263.47美元。 今日美元走强，澳元和新西兰元对美元的跌幅最大。以下是一段周末总结视频，在交易周即将结束之际，概述了这两对货币对的技术面走势。按主要货币对美元的跌幅排序如下：日元下跌0.17%，加元下跌0.19%，英镑下跌0.60%，欧元下跌0.78%，新西兰元下跌1.19%，澳元下跌1.23%。金价对收益率上升和美元走强作出了负面反应。黄金暴跌147.17美元，跌幅达3.29%，创下自3月20日以来的最差单日表现。本周金价累计下跌4.614%。白银下跌6.02美元，跌幅达8.15%（创下自5月15日以来的最差单日表现）。本周价格下跌9.837% 比特币延续跌势，本周跌幅超过16%，创下自2022年10月以来最惨重的一周跌幅 回顾昨日，财政部长贝森特曾表示，他希望就业报告能提前一天发布。尽管他否认对数据有任何预先了解，但事后看来，这一评论显得尤为耐人寻味。讽刺的是，通常被视为利好经济的消息，最终却成了市场的利空。 强于预期的就业报告导致国债收益率大幅攀升，投资者重新评估了美联储近期降息的可能性。结果是股市全面抛售，表现强劲的科技股和人工智能股领跌。这引发了一个有趣的问题：今天是否有些内部人士过得不太顺心？接下来，市场将准备迎接凯文·沃什作为美联储主席的首场会议，但在那之前，下周将公布CPI数据，市场预期核心通胀率将上涨0.5%，同比涨幅将从2.8%升至2.9%。整体通胀率预计将从上月的3.8%升至4.2%。加拿大央行预计将维持利率不变，但鉴于强劲的就业报告，其政策是否会出现转变值得关注。欧洲央行也将召开会议，市场已将25个基点的加息预期计入价格。政策制定者此前已对此释放了相当明确的信号。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。
items.summaryEn: US stocks close sharply lower. The NASDAQ index has it worse trading day since April 2025 Meta is considering raising tens of billion dollars in a stock offering Trump: Would like to see lower interest rates Baker Hughes oil rig count up 2 to 431 Gold is sinking with the US dollar rising and yields moving higher Traders push bitcoin to the lowest level going back to October 2024. Stocks reach new lows CNN: Iran-US talks reportedly deadlocked Energy Sec Wright: We will refill the SPR by 40M barrels after Iran conflict is over Trump cheers Jobs report. Bond yields rising/ stock sliding send warnings Tech meltdown deepens while consumer defensive stocks shine bright Fed's Hammack: Today's jobs report reaffirms that the jobs market is roughly in balance Bitcoin nears the lows of the year and the technical picture is ugly if $60,000 breaks Canada employment change 87.8 K vs 10.0 estimate. The unemployment rate falls to 6.6% US May non-farm payrolls +172K vs +85K expected investingLive European markets wrap: A mixed mood waiting on NFP, US-Iran developments The North American employment picture received a significant boost today as both the United States and Canada delivered much stronger-than-expected labor market reports, reinforcing the view that economic activity remains resilient despite concerns about slowing growth and elevated interest rates. In the United States, nonfarm payrolls increased by 172,000 in May, nearly double the consensus estimate of 85,000. Adding to the strength, prior months were revised higher by a combined 93,000 jobs, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% and wage growth remained firm. The report suggested that hiring momentum remains intact and reduced expectations that the Federal Reserve will be in a position to ease policy anytime soon. Treasury yields surged following the release, the U.S. dollar strengthened, and equity markets came under pressure as investors repriced the outlook for interest rates. Canada's labor market also surprised to the upside. Employment rose by 87,800 jobs versus expectations for a gain of just 10,000, while the unemployment rate fell sharply to 6.6% from 6.9%. The strength was particularly encouraging because it was driven by a surge of 154,000 full-time jobs, offsetting weakness seen earlier in the year. Job gains were broad-based, led by construction, transportation and warehousing, accommodation and food services, information and recreation, and manufacturing. The primary area of weakness remained wholesale and retail trade. Taken together, the reports painted a picture of two labor markets that remain far more resilient than expected. That is the good news. The not so good news for policymakers, is that the stronger employment data reduces pressure for additional monetary easing. In the U.S., markets pushed Treasury yields higher and increased expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates elevated for longer and perhaps raise rates toward the end of the year (that would be a big reversal from just a few months ago). While in Canada the report reinforced expectations that the Bank of Canada may remain on hold after its recent easing cycle. Currency markets reflected the stronger Canadian data, with USDCAD moving modestly lower following the release, although gains in the U.S. dollar from the stronger U.S. report limited the downside. The stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report sparked a sharp selloff in the Treasury market as traders reduced expectations for near-term Federal Reserve rate cuts. The move was led by the front end of the yield curve, reflecting a repricing of Fed policy expectations. The 2-year Treasury yield climbed 10.0 basis points to 4.15%, while the 5-year yield rose 7.9 basis points to 4.268. Longer-term yields also moved higher, with the benchmark 10-year yield increasing 5.5 basis points to 4.530% and the 30-year bond yield advancing 2.0 basis points to 4.996%. The steeper rise in shorter-dated yields highlighted the market's view that a resilient labor market and still-elevated inflation pressures could keep the Federal Reserve on hold for longer than previously anticipated.Stocks were mixed to start the day with the Dow higher and the S&P and Nasdaq lower (Nasdaq was down about 300 points going into the jobs report). The jobs report sent the stocks lower on the back up in yields Concerns about the events of the week with Alphabets floating of $85 billion of equity a reminder that AI is going to cost a lot, and that cost is now eating into shareowners value as equity gets diluted. In the past, stock owners benefited from buybacks of shares reversing dilution.. Now with the number of shares increasing, that idea is reversing The declines started to accelerate with both the S&P and NASDAQ indices closed closing below their 200 hour moving averages for the first time since April 2026. For the S&P index the 200 hour moving average comes in at 7404.33. The closing price was 7383.73. For the NASDAQ index the 200 hour moving averages at 26069.49 with a closing price well below that level at 25709.43. There were a number of losers which fell over 10% today including: In a unique week, Marvel Technology was one of the worst performers today with a decline of -16.74%, but one of the best performers for the week with a gain of 28.52%. Indicative of the craziness, it's stock is still up 210% for the year. The stock price this week reached a $324.20 before closing today at $263.47. The USD was stronger today with the AUD and the NZD the hardest hit vs the greenback. Below is an end of week video, outlining the technicals for those two pairs as the trading week comes to an end. Ranking the major currencies losses versus the greenback showed JPY -0.17% CAD -0.19% GBP -0.60% EUR -0.78% NZD -1.19% AUD -1.23% The price of gold reacted negatively to the higher yields and the higher dollar. Gold tumbled $147.17 or -3.29% for its worst day since March 20. For the week the price fell -4.614% Silver fell by $-6.02 or -8.15% (its worst day since May 15). For the week the price fell -9.837% Bitcoin continued its move to the downside fell more than 16% this week its worst one week % decline since October 2022 Recall from yesterday, Treasury Secretary Bessent remarked that he wished the employment report had been released a day earlier. While he denied having any advance knowledge of the numbers, the comment looks particularly interesting in hindsight. Ironically, what would normally be considered good news for the economy turned out to be bad news for the market. The stronger-than-expected jobs report sent Treasury yields sharply higher as investors reassessed the likelihood of near-term Fed rate cuts. The result was a broad stock market selloff, with high-flying technology and AI shares leading the decline. It raises an interesting question: Did some insiders have a rough day today? The markets will next prepare for Kevin Warsh's first meeting as the Fed chair, but before then, the CPI data will be released next week with expectations for a core gain of 0.5% and the YoY rising to 2.9% from 2.8%. The headline is expected to reach 4.2% from 3.8% last month. The Bank of Canada is expected to keep rates unchanged but with the strong jobs report it will be interesting to see if there is a shift. The ECB will also meet and the market has priced a 25 basis point hike. That has been pretty well telegraphed from policy makers already. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 关键宏观数据超预期时，点差与滑点风险通常放大，建议降低仓位并控制杠杆。
items.impactEn: When macro data surprises, spread/slippage risk often widens; consider smaller position size and tighter leverage control.

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the final Eurozone Q1 GDP report and Italian Retail Sales. The data is not going to change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we have the Canadian Employment report and the US NFP. The Canadian data is expected to show 10K jobs added in May vs -17.7K in April, and the Unemployment Rate to remain unchanged at 6.9%. Most of Canadian data has been surprising to the downside which led traders to pare back the hawkish bets. There's now just 46% chance of a rate hike by year-end. The US NFP is expected at 88K vs 115K in the prior month, and the Unemployment Rate to remain unchanged at 4.3%. The Average Hourly Earnings Y/Y is expected at 3.4% vs 3.6% prior, while the M/M metric is seen at 0.3% vs 0.2% prior. The NFP, and especially the CPI, are likely to influence the tone at the upcoming FOMC meeting. The US jobs data has been consistently surprising to the upside pointing to a stable and even re-accelerating labour market. The Fed projected a 4.4% unemployment rate in 2026, so we are already below that. Their attention has shifted to inflation as that's the part of the mandate more in tension now. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 13:40 GMT/09:40 ET - BoE's Dhingra (dovish - voter) 18:00 GMT/14:00 ET - BoE Governor Bailey (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了欧元区第一季度GDP终值和意大利零售销售等几项低关注度数据外，日程上没有太多值得关注的内容。这些数据不会对欧洲央行的决策产生影响，因此市场反应预计将较为平淡。美股时段 在美股时段，我们将迎来加拿大就业报告和美国非农就业数据。预计加拿大5月将新增1万个就业岗位，而4月为减少1.77万个，失业率将维持在6.9%不变。此前加拿大多项数据表现均低于预期，导致交易员削减了鹰派押注。目前年底前加息的概率仅为46%。美国非农就业数据预计为8.8万，低于上月的11.5万，失业率预计维持在4.3%不变。预计平均时薪同比增速为3.4%，低于前值3.6%；环比增速预计为0.3%，高于前值0.2%。非农就业数据，尤其是CPI数据，很可能影响即将召开的FOMC会议基调。美国就业数据一直持续好于预期，表明劳动力市场保持稳定，甚至可能再次加速增长。 美联储曾预测2026年失业率为4.4%，因此当前失业率已低于该水平。随着通胀问题成为当前政策使命中更为紧迫的环节，美联储的关注重点已转向通胀。央行官员讲话格林尼治时间13:40/美国东部时间09:40 - 英格兰银行的丁格拉（鸽派 - 投票委员）格林尼治时间18:00/美国东部时间14:00 - 英格兰银行行长贝利（中性 - 投票委员）本文由investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔撰写。
items.summaryEn: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the final Eurozone Q1 GDP report and Italian Retail Sales. The data is not going to change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we have the Canadian Employment report and the US NFP. The Canadian data is expected to show 10K jobs added in May vs -17.7K in April, and the Unemployment Rate to remain unchanged at 6.9%. Most of Canadian data has been surprising to the downside which led traders to pare back the hawkish bets. There's now just 46% chance of a rate hike by year-end. The US NFP is expected at 88K vs 115K in the prior month, and the Unemployment Rate to remain unchanged at 4.3%. The Average Hourly Earnings Y/Y is expected at 3.4% vs 3.6% prior, while the M/M metric is seen at 0.3% vs 0.2% prior. The NFP, and especially the CPI, are likely to influence the tone at the upcoming FOMC meeting. The US jobs data has been consistently surprising to the upside pointing to a stable and even re-accelerating labour market. The Fed projected a 4.4% unemployment rate in 2026, so we are already below that. Their attention has shifted to inflation as that's the part of the mandate more in tension now. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 13:40 GMT/09:40 ET - BoE's Dhingra (dovish - voter) 18:00 GMT/14:00 ET - BoE Governor Bailey (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Drone attack on Oman raises the stakes
items.summary: Explosion halts crude loading at Oman's Mina al Fahal in conflict escalation PBOC resumes injections after two-day pause that forced banks to deploy idle cash Nasdaq analysis today shows market started selling quite hard for this time of day RBA's Hauser due to speak, hawkish tilt expected after Harper's inflation warning PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8157 (vs. estimate at 6.7735) South Korean markets hammered. KOSPI off 6%, won at a 17 year low vs. USD Japan renews yen intervention threat as reserves fall by record amount in May Fake news? : Trump officials in talks to take government equity stakes in US AI giants Japan fin min Katayama said ready to respond to FX. Yen shrugs it off. Iran threatens US bases and Hormuz as war talks drag with no deal in sight Japan's real wages rise for fourth month, strengthening the BOJ rate-hike case SpaceX faces full S&P 500 wait as index giant rejects fast-track entry rules Goldman tells clients to buy US equity dips even as risk appetite hits highest since 2021 ICYMI: OPEC says oil demand remains strong despite Hormuz, Mid East conflict surging price All crypto eyes on Strategy's Monday disclosure as bitcoin seeks a floor after brutal week investingLive Americas market news wrap: Another comeback for stocks as Trump talks peace Dow closes at a record high. S&P is higher, and the NASDAQ index is down on the day Summary: Explosion reported near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal on the Gulf of Oman; cause unclear but an alleged drone attack has been cited, extending conflict risk beyond the Strait of Hormuz to open-water export infrastructure, oil tracked broadly sideways Japan April real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month, the longest such streak in five years; wages and household spending both beat forecasts, strengthening the BOJ's case for a rate hike at its June 15-16 meeting Finance Minister Katayama renewed yen intervention warnings with USD/JPY around 160; the pair barely moved as dollar trading was broadly subdued ahead of US payrolls later Friday Japanese equities fell, with souring tech sentiment cited as the primary drag South Korea's KOSPI deepened losses, falling 6+%, as the Korean won hit its weakest level against the dollar since March 2009; a Labour Minister suggestion that Samsung and AI-boom beneficiaries share excess profits with workers echoed the kind of official commentary that triggered a previous KOSPI slide Notus reported that senior Trump administration officials have held discussions with major AI companies including OpenAI about voluntarily ceding equity stakes to the government, with returns potentially distributed to American citizens as an AI dividend Hong Kong equities fell after Beijing moved to tighten cross-border capital outflow controls; mainland Chinese stocks held up better, supported by the PBOC resuming open market operations Asian markets closed out the week on a cautious note, with South Korean equities bearing the sharpest losses as a confluence of domestic political signals and broader risk-off sentiment drove the KOSPI down >6% at one stage. The Korean won deepened its slide against the dollar, hitting its weakest level since March 2009, as selling pressure intensified through the session. The proximate trigger was a suggestion from South Korea's Labour Minister that Samsung and other companies riding the AI boom should consider distributing a share of the excess profits those gains have produced. It was the kind of official commentary that has rattled Korean markets before, and traders did not wait to find out whether it would be followed by policy action. The AI profit-sharing theme found an unlikely parallel in a report from US outlet Notus, which revealed that senior Trump administration officials have held preliminary discussions with major artificial intelligence companies, including OpenAI, about the government acquiring voluntary equity stakes in their firms. Under the concept as described, those shareholdings could eventually generate returns distributed directly to American citizens in the form of an AI dividend. The talks are at an early stage and the legal pathway remains unclear, but the emergence of the idea from both Washington and Seoul in the same session underlines how politically charged the distribution of AI wealth is becoming. Elsewhere in the region, an explosion near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal added a new dimension to the conflict risk picture. The terminal sits on the Gulf of Oman, outside the Persian Gulf, and an alleged drone attack there, if confirmed, would mark a visible extension of infrastructure targeting beyond the Strait of Hormuz into open-water export facilities. Oman's role as a back-channel intermediary in US-Iran negotiations gives the incident additional diplomatic weight, and it drew immediate scrutiny from energy traders, shipping markets, and analysts tracking the conflict's geographic spread. Oil itself tracked broadly sideways, suggesting markets are still waiting for confirmation of cause and impact before repricing. In Japan, the data flow was constructive. Real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month in April, the longest rising streak in five years, with both nominal earnings and household spending beating forecasts by a meaningful margin. The dataset strengthens the Bank of Japan's hand ahead of its June 15-16 policy meeting, where a rate hike signal is increasingly in play. Finance Minister Katayama renewed her warning that Tokyo stands ready to take decisive action on the yen, with USD/JPY hovering around 160, though the pair barely moved as the dollar was broadly subdued in pre-payroll trade. Japanese equities fell, with tech sentiment souring in the wake of the KOSPI selloff and broader caution around the AI trade. Hong Kong stocks declined after Beijing moved to tighten controls on cross-border capital outflows, while mainland Chinese markets held up more steadily, supported by the PBOC's return to open market operations after a two-day pause that had been read as a deliberate push to redirect idle bank reserves toward the real economy. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 阿曼米纳法哈尔港发生爆炸，原油装船作业暂停，冲突持续升级。中国人民银行在暂停两天后恢复注资，此前暂停曾迫使银行动用闲置现金。纳斯达克今日分析显示，市场在这一时段出现较大幅度的抛售。澳大利亚储备银行的豪泽即将发表讲话，在哈珀发出通胀警告后，预计其将采取鹰派立场。中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8157 （市场预期为6.7735）韩国股市重挫。KOSPI指数下跌6%，韩元兑美元汇率跌至17年低点。日本因5月外汇储备创纪录下降，再次威胁干预日元汇率。假新闻？：特朗普政府官员正商讨由政府入股美国人工智能巨头。日本财务大臣片山表示准备应对外汇市场。日元对此不以为意。 伊朗威胁美军基地和霍尔木兹海峡，而战争谈判陷入僵局，尚无达成协议的迹象 日本实际工资连续第四个月上涨，强化了日本央行加息的理由 SpaceX面临全面等待标普500指数的局面，因该指数巨头拒绝了快速入围规则 高盛建议客户逢低买入美股，尽管市场风险偏好已升至2021年以来最高水平 错过了吗： 欧佩克称尽管霍尔木兹海峡及中东冲突推高油价，但石油需求依然强劲 加密货币市场聚焦Strategy周一披露，比特币在惨烈一周后寻求底部支撑 investingLive美洲市场新闻综述：特朗普谈和平，股市再度反弹，道指收于历史新高。 标普指数上涨，纳斯达克指数当日下跌 摘要：据报道，阿曼湾的阿曼米纳法哈尔原油终端附近发生爆炸； 起因尚不明确，但有消息称系无人机袭击所致，这将冲突风险从霍尔木兹海峡延伸至公海出口基础设施，油价整体呈横盘整理态势；日本4月实际工资连续第四个月上涨，创五年来最长连涨纪录；工资和家庭支出均好于预期，强化了日本央行在6月15日至16日会议上加息的理由；随着美元兑日元汇率徘徊在160附近，财务大臣片山重明再次发出日元干预警告； 受周五晚间美国非农数据公布前美元交易普遍清淡影响，该汇率几乎未见波动；日本股市下跌，科技板块情绪恶化被视为主要拖累因素；韩国KOSPI指数跌幅扩大，下跌逾6%，韩元兑美元汇率跌至2009年3月以来的最低水平； 韩国劳工部长建议三星及人工智能热潮的受益企业与员工分享超额利润，此类官方言论曾引发此前KOSPI指数的下跌；Notus报道称，特朗普政府高级官员已与主要人工智能公司进行了磋商 包括OpenAI在内的多家公司正考虑自愿向政府转让股权，所得收益或将作为“人工智能红利”分配给美国公民；在中央政府收紧跨境资本流出管控后，香港股市下跌； 受中国人民银行恢复公开市场操作支撑，中国内地股市表现相对稳健；亚洲股市本周收盘时情绪谨慎，韩国股市跌幅最大，国内政治信号与整体避险情绪叠加，一度导致KOSPI指数下跌逾6%。随着盘中抛售压力加剧，韩元兑美元汇率跌势加剧，触及2009年3月以来的最低水平。直接导火索是韩国劳动部长提议，三星及其他借AI热潮获利的企业应考虑分配部分由此产生的超额利润。此类官方言论此前曾多次引发韩国市场动荡，交易员们并未等待后续政策动向的确认便已采取行动。 关于人工智能利润分享的议题，在美国媒体Notus的一份报道中意外找到了呼应。该报道披露，特朗普政府的高级官员已与包括OpenAI在内的主要人工智能公司进行了初步讨论，商讨政府以自愿方式收购这些公司股权的事宜。根据报道所述的构想，这些股权最终产生的收益将以“人工智能股息”的形式直接分配给美国公民。谈判尚处于初期阶段，法律路径尚不明确，但华盛顿和首尔在同一会议上同时提出这一构想，凸显了人工智能财富分配问题正变得多么具有政治敏感性。在该地区其他地方，阿曼米纳·法哈尔原油终端附近发生的一起爆炸事件，为冲突风险图景增添了新的维度。该终端位于波斯湾外的阿曼湾，如果据称的无人机袭击得到证实，这将标志着针对基础设施的袭击范围已从霍尔木兹海峡延伸至公海出口设施。 阿曼作为美伊谈判幕后渠道的中介所扮演的角色，使此次事件具有更重的外交分量，并立即引起了能源交易商、航运市场以及关注冲突地域扩散情况的分析师的密切关注。油价整体呈横盘整理态势，表明市场仍在等待事件原因和影响的确认，才进行重新定价。在日本，经济数据表现积极。4月实际工资连续第四个月上涨，创下五年来的最长连涨纪录，名义收入和家庭支出均大幅超出预期。这一数据增强了日本央行在6月15日至16日政策会议前的底气，届时市场对加息信号的预期正日益升温。尽管美元在非农数据公布前的交易中整体表现疲软，美元兑日元汇率仍徘徊在160附近，日本财务大臣片山重明再次警告称，日本政府已准备好对日元采取果断行动。受韩国KOSPI指数抛售以及市场对AI板块普遍持谨慎态度影响，科技股情绪转差，日股因此下跌。 在中央政府采取措施收紧跨境资本外流管控后，香港股市出现下跌；而中国内地股市则表现更为稳健，这得益于中国人民银行在暂停两天后重启公开市场操作——此前市场普遍认为，此次暂停是旨在将闲置的银行储备资金引导至实体经济的刻意之举。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。
